It provides guidance for reporting issues via ubuntu-bugtool and references existing bug reports on Launchpad, with instruction to report module-specific bugs to their respective packages. Teal's Express was a family-owned less-than-truckload (LTL) carrier serving the northeastern United States with headquarters in Watertown, NY, at its peak operating 9 terminals in 4 states; the company closed in August 2022. Its history begins with John Teal and his son Nicholas in the Adirondack Region post-C Civil War, with John later purchasing and operating weekly trips into Utica by 1926 and daily Utica transports by 1928; a 1934 contract to haul paper from Lyons Falls Paper Mills to the Post-Standard in Syracuse spurred growth in Central New York. By 1951 Teal's Express expanded to Watertown and Dexter, delivering food and produce to local shops at night; John Teal Sr. died in 1962, with Mary and children continuing the business. In 1976 authority extended to Syracuse, and in 1980 expanded to Albany, Rochester, and Massena amid deregulation. In 1990 Teal's entered Buffalo and gained Canadian authority for international service; in 1992 daily Northeast service began with bases in Massachusetts, Pennsylvania, and New Jersey. A 2000 maintenance facility adjacent to Utica Terminal supported 24-hour service. In 2006 computer system upgrades provided online shipment tracing and rating data. In 2008 the Jersey City terminal relocated to a larger facility; a 2011 Philadelphia facility was opened; in 2012 a 35-door facility and warehouse in Pittston, PA; in 2016 Apollo Motor Express was acquired (Massachusetts). The content notes Teal's appreciation to customers and staff, and identifies Teal's as a recognized leader in the transport sector.
